name: HCP-Development (Lifespan Human Connectome Project in Development)
description: A large developmental extension of the Human Connectome Project: high-quality multimodal MRI (resting and task fMRI, structural, diffusion) from more than 1,300 children and adolescents ages 5-21 across four U.S. sites, including an emotion-processing task and rich cognitive and social-emotional measures. A key resource for charting how social and emotional brain systems mature. Distributed through the NIMH Data Archive.
citation: Somerville LH, Bookheimer SY, Buckner RL, et al. (2018). The Lifespan Human Connectome Project in Development: A large-scale study of brain connectivity development in 5-21 year olds. NeuroImage, 183, 456-468.
